attacking_style: Employs war-of-attrition tactics with repeated accelerations in final 8km of mountain stages, typically breaking companions around 2-3km from finish after multiple probing attacks have weakened rivals.
team_tactics: Mitchelton-Scott uses dual-threat strategy, allowing Chavez breakaway freedom on mountain stages while preserving domestiques for GC leader. Team sometimes abandons him on rolling/plateau terrain to prioritize resources.
terrain_preference: Excels on steep mountain finishes where repeated accelerations pay off; technical descending skills compensate for climbing deficits. Exposed and fragile on rolling plateaus, long time trials, and sustained tempo efforts.
vulnerability: Complete collapse in long TT efforts (4+ minutes lost). Vulnerable when Sky/strong teams set high sustained tempo on rolling terrain—team abandons him in these scenarios, revealing tactical acceptance of weakness.
key_patterns: Repeated attacks in final 8km until rivals crack, not single decisive move (3 observations)
Immediately follows top GC threats' attacks but cannot sustain multi-day defense (3 observations)
Strong technical descending recovers time/contact lost on climbs (2 observations)
Mental resilience: continues attacking after failures, doesn't abandon aggression (2 observations)
Team deliberately sacrifices him on rolling plateau stages to save domestiques (2 observations)
Time trial efforts reveal inability to sustain power beyond climbing efforts (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2021 showed strong closing speed on climbs (7s gap closed) but no wins since 2019 stage victory. Gap between observations limits assessment.
